channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: FFXIV Is Actually, Seriously Doing A Jollibee Collab Promo Where You Earn An In-Game Chicken Eating Emote
item.content:encoded:
Once again, the critically-acclaimed MMORPG Final Fantasy 14 has a promotion that’s taken the community by surprise. In North American and Canada, Square Enix has teamed up with Jollibee, of all places, to sell specialty meals and merchandise for an in-game reward.

Starting this coming Tuesday, April 21, those who pick up whatever this promotion is putting down will get the “Eat Chicken” emote, which not only has the player eating a piece of chicken, but also holding a (thankfully unbranded) bucket as well. 

For those willing to have a whole meal for the privilege, there’ll be two options. The first, more geared toward individuals, will be a variety box meal that features a chicken sandwich, a piece of chicken, a side, a peach mango pie, and a specialty drink—either the Blueberry Raspberry Aether with Crystal Boba drink in America, or the classic Pineapple Quencher in Canada. 

The second option for the whole household (or one very enthusiastic chicken eater) is a bucket bundle, which includes six pieces of chicken and three of the aforementioned specialty drinks, either the Blueberry Raspberry Aether with Crystal Boba drink in America, or the Pineapple Quencher in Canada.

During the promo dates between April 21 and May 31, and assumedly so long as there are supplies, you’ll get the code as long as you get the chosen deal, regardless of acquisition method. 

The other option will be through a merchandise collaboration with popular clothing brand Complex (which is one heck of a combination), which features images such as the Jollibee himself riding a chocobo. 

Once you get a code, as described on the Lodestone, you’ll have to go to the code redemption page on Jollibee’s site (which isn’t live yet at the time of writing, but may be when the promo launches) to put the code from your purchase into. Do this by November 30, and you’ll be given a code for the emote. Notably, this only appears to give you one code for a single character, so if you have alts, you might have to eat quite a lot of chicken. More details are outlined on the Lodestone page. 

While this chain is from the Philippines, this FFXIV promotion unfortunately only seems to be in North America and Canada, which understandably has some actual Filipinos upset. (This also happened with the Gong Cha promotion, where other Southeast Asian countries had it but the Philippines didn’t.) Hopefully they get their moment. 

It’s not too great for a bulk of America, either, though—the chain lists only 83 stores as Open across 15 states, with 35 in California alone (likely due to the high Asian population), 7 in New York City, 5 in Hawaii (which has many Filipinos) and, somehow, 6 in New Jersey (which, as a New Jersey Filipino myself—yeah). Canada’s a little more dire yet, with only 28 locations across the massive country, 12 of which are in Ontario alone and 8 in Alberta. Unfortunately, this also means the Jollibee near the Vatican is also excluded. 

This comes right before the start of the FFXIV Fan Festival in Anaheim, where there happens to be a Jollibee that’ll be decked out in themed stuff for the festival itself (and is open from 8:00 a.m. until 11:00 p.m., which means I’ll probably be getting breakfast there, honestly). All of this also preempts the start of the final Dawntrail patches, which saw a Live Letter showing off gameplay, explaining more details and unveiling this promotion. 

[Author’s Note: Also, I swear I’m not biased because I’m Filipino, but Jollibee really is that good. Almost as good as Popeyes. Definitely better than KFC, which also makes it leagues better than CFA.] 

Victoria Rose

Victoria has professionally written about games since 2016, including small former tenures with Polygon and Fanbyte. She mostly spends time in FFXIV, head-deep in roleplay campaigns or stubbornly playing Black Mage through high-end raids. Former obsessions include Dota 2 and The Secret World (also mostly roleplaying). Come visit their estate: Diabolos (Crystal DC), Goblet, Ward 4, Plot 28.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/ffxiv-is-actually-seriously-doing-a-jollibee-collab-promo-where-you-earn-an-in-game-chicken-eating-emote-2000137840
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Asterfel Opens Playtest on Steam, Promises Old-School ARPG Vibes
item.content:encoded:
Looking for something to play this weekend? If you want a new ARPG to hop into and spent your formative gaming years lost in the worlds of Gothic or Risen, Asterfel might be worth a spin as their playtest has just gone live.

Developer Mysteria Studio UG has opened a playtest for their upcoming action RPG on Steam. The team includes a former level designer from Gothic 3 and Risen, so the "inspired by classic 2000s RPGs" pitch carries a bit more weight than your average indie talking point. It wasn't long ago we reported on the announcement of the game, and now it's finally time for you to get your hands on it - albeit temporarily.

As a little prelude, players will be whisked away to the island of Asterfel, and as many RPGs do, you'll be positioned as a shipwrecked outsider dropped into a world dealing with some serious problems. The King's magical mining operation has awakened a slumbering ancient god, mutated the local wildlife, and stirred up the undead for good measure. Nature and magic are both going haywire, and nobody's exactly thrilled about it.

Three factions are vying for control of the island: The Crown Wardens serve the King's interests, The Kindred push back against exploitative mining practices, and The Scholars are laser-focused on understanding what magic is actually doing to the land. You can pick a side, play them all, or chart your own path entirely, outlining the open nature of the game.

Combat covers a range of fantasy styles from swords and bows, to magical rune-casting, with weapon crafting a focal point, letting you tailor your loadout to your playstyle. The open world scales with distance, meaning the further you push into the island, the more difficult things get.

But all that said, Asterfel does not yet have a release window. That won't stop you from playing, however, as potential players can request playtest access now via the game's Steam page, and pop in for a spell before the playtest ends. Don't forget to join the official Discord to speak with other players, and provide feedback to the developers as you go!



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/asterfel-opens-playtest-on-steam-promises-old-school-arpg-vibes-2000137841
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Peter Molyneux's 'God Game' Masters of Albion Arrives in Early Access April 22
item.content:encoded:
Peter Molyneux and 22cans are finally ready to put Masters of Albion out there for all to play. The 'god game' launches into Steam Early Access on April 22, 2026, and builds on Molyneux vision of a living world controlled by you. 

If you've been out of the loop on Masters of Albion, the latest Molyneux title is an attempt to distill everything he built across Black & White, Dungeon Keeper, and the Fable series into a single modern package. The game loop cycles with having you build and manage a fully customizable town and work through the game's narrative. As night creeps in, creatures descend on that same town and you defend it in real-time combat, using whatever powers you've assembled to keep things standing until morning. It's a deliberately old-school loop dressed up in contemporary design.

The team behind it carries serious pedigree. Alongside Molyneux, 22cans brought in Fable composer Russell Shaw, LittleBigPlanet and Media Molecule co-founder Mark Healey, artist Kareem Ettouney, and writer Iain Wright, the same crew whose fingerprints are on a lot of the Lionhead catalog that fans of the era still talk about.

We covered the Masters of Albion announcement back in 2024, when Molyneux openly discussed using profits from his NFT title Legacy to fund the project and freely acknowledged his history of overpromising. The candor was appreciated, if nothing else. With his new game on the horizon, we can only hope it delivers on everything the trailers have shown - and more. 

Players who buy and play within the first week of Early Access will receive a Founders Paint Pack, an exclusive set of in-game colors for early supporters. The game is available now on Steam priced at $24.99 with a 10% early adopter discount at launch.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/peter-molyneuxs-god-game-masters-of-albion-arrives-in-early-access-april-22-2000137839
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Torchlight: Infinite SS12 Lunaria Is Live: New Hero, New Farming Loop, and Two Permanent Systems
item.content:encoded:
Torchlight: Infinite SS12 Lunaria is now live on PC and mobile, and XD Games is wasting no time making the game's third anniversary count. We recently detailed the new season, which brings the Lunaria farming loop, a new hero, and a pair of permanent additions that are sticking around well past the season's end.

The seasonal mechanic centers on Lunar Stone Statues scattered across Netherrealm. Players wielding Lunar Rings awaken these statues to summon Lunari creatures, and defeating them drops both loot and energy to keep the chain going. Randomly triggered Lunar Omens and Rhapsodies mix things up mid-run, with effects ranging from spawning a golden statue loaded with better loot to resetting all Lunari creatures back into statues.

New hero Scent Weaver Sage is available now as well. She's an elixir-based fighter who tweaks her recipes mid-combat and can pick up defensive stats not typically found on gear. Her arrival also brings a full potion system overhaul, rebranded as Elixir Skills, which now run on charges rather than mana.

On the permanent side, Creation Engines are live across the map. Clear the guarding enemies, and nearby Creation Crystals convert into high-end loot, with Netherrealm Talent investment unlocking stronger options. The Modularization system is also in, letting players summon a copy of a recently defeated boss to fight alongside them, with 20 bosses available at launch.

For those looking to get a full rundown before they jump in, you can check out the full patch notes on the official site.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/torchlight-infinite-ss12-lunaria-is-live-new-hero-new-farming-loop-and-two-permanent-systems-2000137838
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
